channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: The stars have come out at the Kennedy Center for Coppola, the Grateful Dead, Raitt and Sandoval
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Celebrities, cultural icons and a few surprise guests have gathered for the annual Kennedy Center Honors celebration in Washington on Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: ASHRAF KHALIL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says he can't guarantee tariffs won't raise US prices and won't rule out revenge prosecutions
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump said he can't guarantee that his promised tariffs on key U.S. foreign trade partners won't raise prices for American consumers and he suggested once more that some political rivals and federal officials who pursued legal cases against him should be imprisoned.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Assad arrives in Moscow, is granted asylum by Russia
item.description: Syrian dictator Bashar Assad has arrived in Moscow and was granted asylum by the Russian government, a longtime ally of the Syrian leader in the nearly 14-year-long civil war.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democrats say they'll avoid election challenges on Jan. 6
item.description: House Democrats say that they’ll skip the election protests they’ve staged on Jan. 6 in past presidential cycles, four years after supporters of President-elect Trump stormed the Capitol in an attempt to interrupt the certification of the 2020 election results.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says US should stay out of fighting in Syria as opposition forces gain
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump said Saturday that the U.S. military should stay out of the fast-escalating conflict in Syria, where a dramatic rebel offensive reached the capital and threatened the rule of Syria's Russian- and Iranian-allied president.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, WILL WEISSERT and TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden administration announces $988M in aid to Ukraine on same day Trump meets with Zelenskyy in Paris
item.description: The Biden administration announced $988 million in aid to Ukraine after the president promised to give as much aid to the country as possible before President-elect Trump takes office.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Clintons urge voters agitated by today's politics to remain involved in public service
item.description: Bill and Hillary Clinton say voters disappointed with the presidential election need to remain involved in public service and find ways to give back.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Demillo, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump's new coalition puts female nominees at forefront
item.description: Women are set to play a key role in President-elect Trump’s administration, as he nominates and appoints a number of them to high-level posts. Trump has so far named eight women to Cabinet-level positions — double the four initially named for his first term.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Trump meets with Macron, Zelenskyy ahead of Notre Dame reopening ceremony in Paris
item.description: President-elect Trump visited with French President Emmanuel Macron and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy ahead of the Notre Dame reopening ceremony.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Election Day has long passed. In some states, legislatures are working to undermine the results
item.description: Voters in some parts of the country are discovering that having their say at the ballot box is not necessarily the final word, even though this year's election was over a month ago.
item.dc:creator: Gary Fields, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Bureau of Prisons agrees to court monitor, public acknowledgement of staff-on-inmate sexual abuse
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A legal settlement in the wake of rampant staff-on-inmate sexual abuse will force the federal Bureau of Prisons to open its doors to a court-appointed monitor and publicly acknowledge pervasive misconduct at its now-shuttered women’s prison in California.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and MICHAEL BALSAMO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump offers a public show of support for Hegseth and says he believes he can be confirmed
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump on Friday offered a public show of support for Pete Hegseth, his embattled choice to lead the Defense Department, whose confirmation by the Senate is in doubt as he faces questions over allegations of excessive drinking, sexual assault and his views on
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG and JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump taps forceful ally of hard-line immigration policies to head Customs and Border Protection
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The picture of who will be in charge of executing President-elect Donald Trump's hard-line immigration and border policies has come into sharper focus after he announced his picks to head Customs and Border Protection and also the agency tasked with deporting immigrants in the coun
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: On pardons, Biden weighs whether to flex presidential powers in broad new ways
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden faces a stark choice as he contemplates broad preemptive pardons to protect aides and allies from potential retribution by Donald Trump: Does he hew to the institutional norms he’s spent decades defending or flex the powers of the presidency in untested ways? Th
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG, ZEKE MILLER and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vance tours Helene damage in North Carolina
item.description: The hurricane struck in late September and caused at least $53 billion in damage in North Carolina, according to a state government estimate.
item.dc:creator: Keleigh Beeson, NewsNation

item.title: Vance tells residents in hurricane-stricken North Carolina that they haven't been forgotten
item.description: FAIRVIEW, N.C. (AP) — Vice President-elect JD Vance on Friday assured residents of western North Carolina still cleaning up from Hurricane Helene that they haven't been forgotten as he surveyed storm wreckage and talked to first responders in one of his first public appearances since the election.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE and GARY ROBERTSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden weighs preemptive pardons before leaving office
item.description: President Joe Biden is weighing sweeping pardons for allies, who the White House believes could be targeted by President-elect Donald Trump’s incoming administration, according to people familiar with the matter.
item.dc:creator: Vinay Simlot, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ocasio-Cortez seeks to lead Democrats on Oversight panel
item.description: Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (D-N.Y.) on Friday formally launched a bid to lead Democrats on the Oversight and Accountability Committee in the next Congress — a powerful seat that will play a prominent role in the party's effort to push back against President Trump in his second term.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, Fox 5

item.title: Former national security officials urge caution on Gabbard
item.description: A group of nearly 100 former national security officials urged the Senate to “carefully scrutinize” former Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii), President-elect Trump’s nominee to serve as director of national intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: A court upholds the U.S. ban on TikTok
item.description: The appeals court agreed that the viral video app must be sold off by its China-based parent company, rejecting TikTok's claim that the crackdown violates the free speech rights of millions of Americans.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn, MPR News

item.title: NYC plan would abolish income tax for certain residents: See if you qualify
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – A new proposal would abolish New York City’s personal income tax for nearly half a million qualifying residents.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11

item.title: Hegseth faces senators' concerns not only about his behavior but also his views on women in combat
item.description: Minnesotan Pete Hegseth has spent the week on Capitol Hill trying to reassure Republican senators that he is fit to lead President-elect Donald Trump’s Department of Defense in the wake of high-profile allegations about excessive drinking and sexual assault.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Head of Democratic Caucus says thin House margins make attendance vital next year
item.description: The head of the House Democratic Caucus is sending an early message to his troops heading into the 119th Congress: Attendance will be crucial given the razor-tight margins in the lower chamber. “It’s important for every member to come to work and to do their job.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: St. Cloud appoints first Somali American to city council
item.description: On a unanimous vote, the city council appointed Hudda Ibrahim to fill a vacancy on the seven-member board. She replaces Jake Anderson, who was elected St. Cloud mayor last month.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: Ocasio-Cortez first person to hit a million followers on Bluesky
item.description: The platform’s features are based off of Twitter, and they include the ability to message fellow users as well as a discover and feed tab.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Biden’s pardon of son Hunter threatens to tarnish legacy
item.description: President Biden’s decision to pardon his son Hunter in his final full month in the White House is threatening to erode his legacy as he prepares to leave office.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: At Supreme Court, vaping may be frowned upon, but that could change with Trump
item.description: The focal point of the case is 2009 law enacted by Congress that gives the Food and Drug Administration a mandate to curb the availability of nicotine products for minors.
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg, MPR News

item.title: Hegseth forced out of vets groups due to drinking, misconduct: New Yorker
item.description: The incidents — relayed in a trail of documents and corroborated to The New Yorker by the accounts of former colleagues — took place prior to Hegseth becoming a full-time Fox News TV host in 2017.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, Fox 5
